IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We regularly inspect our electrical equipment to ensure it is safe and efficient. We need to examine a transformer on our electricity poles to check it is functioning properly. This essential work helps to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned power cuts in the future as we appreciate you may have been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your electricity. We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network in the future. This work is taking place in the Clayhill Lane area.

This work is taking place near Orchard Estate. Two way traffic lights will be in place to allow the works to be carried out safely.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified a pole and the associated equipment that need replacing and upgrading. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. To allow our contractors to safely replace poles in your area, we will need to temporarily disconnect the generator on 1st June 2026. Once the work on 1st June 2026 is finished, the temporary generator will be reconnected. After all planned works have been completed, we will then disconnect the generator on 5th June 2026 and return your supply to the main power network. This work is taking place near Harwich Road the B1414 in Great Oakley.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We are needing to carry out some maintenance work on a piece of electrical equipment known as a transformer. This is due to homes and businesses adopting low-carbon technologies such as electric vehicles, heat pumps and renewable generation which means the energy demand on the local network is changing. We need to turn off your power so we can carry out a tap change which will change the voltage settings at the transformer to help provide additional capacity for low carbon technologies, improve efficiency and reduce energy losses and help support a cleaner and more sustainable energy network. This will help the needs for net zero now and in the future. This will improve the reliability of our electricity network and ensure it is working to the best of its ability to support net zero. This work should be completed within 15 minutes of the power being turned off, however we like to provide a wider restriction window in case our engineers encounter any issues on the day. This is taking place on Pentstemon Drive.

Sent by Theo at 02:44 - Thank you for your patience while our engineers made repairs to a fault on our network in the Coddenham Road area, we've now restored power to most of the affected properties. Please call us on 08003163105 (free from landlines and mobiles).

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
